lyrics:
Joni don't listen to the things they say to you
Don't listen when they say you're foolish
Don't listen when they're being cruel

He left you and you couldn't stand it
So you went looking for another plan
It's not my place to give advice but
I don't think you need a man

What you need is a friend

Joni you're beautiful
Joni you're beautiful
you're beautiful

Joni stop crying
We all have crosses we have to bear
It's not your fault that you've been hurt, girl
But who says that life is fair

I know you're feeling sad and lonely
And you're missing the touch of his hand
But maybe you should take a moment
'cause I don't think you need a man

What you need is a friend

Joni you're beautiful
Joni you're beautiful
you're beautiful

Joni you're beautiful
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This is a high-energy, anthemic rock track with an empowering and slightly defiant emotional atmosphere. The production is dense and polished, featuring a wide wall of heavily distorted guitars and a punchy, driving drum kit that creates a powerful "wall of sound." The lead vocal sits clearly on top of the mix, supported by prominent harmonies, and the overall feel is tight and commercially ready. A defining moment is the melodic and technically proficient guitar solo that erupts after the second chorus, perfectly elevating the track's emotional peak.

review.review_text: "Joni" hits with the kind of direct sincerity that feels rare in rock songwriting—a friend’s voice cutting through the noise, telling someone they’re beautiful not because it’s easy, but because it needs to be said. Built on a galloping 161 BPM foundation and anchored in A minor, the track layers distorted guitars into a wall that never overwhelms the vocal; instead, it props up the tenderness of the message. PCBender’s harmonic support and that technically assured guitar solo after the second chorus elevate what could have been a simple verse-chorus into something more architecturally sound—the production knows when to surge and when to let the lyric breathe. It’s a song about choosing friendship over false fixes, and the music refuses to cheapen that choice with sentimentality. A highlight that proves PCBender can marry craft with heart without sacrificing either.
anchors_used: guitar solo eruption after second chorus
"What you need is a friend" as central argument
